# Omni-Agent Phase 4D — Multimodal Input Pipeline (Telegram + LINE)
<!-- Generated by conv2spec | Source: conversation | Date: 2026-04-29 -->
<!-- Type: feature_ | Target consumer: Jules (Google AI dev agent) -->

## Project Context

Cindy currently understands only text. Family members regularly send images, voice messages, stickers, and GIFs through Telegram and LINE — all of which Cindy ignores or fails to interpret. This phase adds multimodal input handling for Telegram and LINE, using Gemini 2.5 Flash/Pro as the unified multimodal backend. BlueBubbles is explicitly frozen at text-only; iMessage multimodal will be solved later via a separate iPhone SE + Apple Intelligence embodiment phase (Phase 4E). Phase 4D is the immediate prerequisite for Phase 5 (Family Preference Awareness), since preference learning needs clean multimodal signal from existing channels.

## Scope

**In scope:** Telegram inline media (photo, voice, audio, sticker, animation/GIF), LINE inline media (image, audio, sticker), Gemini-bound multimodal routing, two-stage vision (OCR fast path → Gemini multimodal escalation), voice transcript persistence, sticker-via-vision unified path, fixed-template multimodal acknowledgment ("嗯,收到了,正在看/聽..."), GIF first-frame prompt hint to Gemini, honest fallback messaging on Gemini failure, ModelRouter modality dimension, `omni-workspace` reuse for media downloads, 120hr cleanup extended to media files.

**Out of scope:** BlueBubbles attachment handling (frozen, text-only maintained), iMessage / FaceTime integration (deferred to Phase 4E), Whisper / Google STT preprocessing layer (Gemini native audio only), Claude / local LLM multimodal fallback (intentionally not pursued), short video full-frame analysis (GIF first-frame only), advanced PDF chart understanding (still deferred from Phase 4B), sticker pack metadata lookup tables (intentionally skipped for path consistency), child-specific content filtering / SOUL.md updates for child interaction (deferred to Phase 4E or Phase 5), LLM-generated personality-driven acknowledgments (4D uses fixed templates only — personality-driven reactions belong in Phase 5/6 with SOUL.md and AAAK).

**Assumptions:** Gemini 2.5 Flash and Pro both support image and audio inputs in current API contract, Telegram Bot API and LINE Messaging API media download tokens remain valid for at least the time required to download to `omni-workspace`, `omni-workspace` shared volume from Phase 4B is healthy and mounted into both Gateway and Brain, OAuth Gemini client from Phase 4C handles token refresh transparently for multimodal calls, family members consent to voice transcripts being persisted to PostgreSQL.

---

## Functional Requirements

### Task: Extend StandardMessage.Attachment for inline media

**Requirement:** The Go Gateway's `StandardMessage.Attachment` struct must carry enough metadata for the Brain to dispatch correctly across image, voice, and sticker modalities.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given a `StandardMessage` with an attached image, when serialized for the Brain, then the payload contains `media_type=image`, `mime_type`, `local_path` under `/workspace`, and source platform identifier.
- [ ] Given a voice message, when serialized, then the payload contains `media_type=voice`, `mime_type`, `duration_ms`, and `local_path`.
- [ ] Given a sticker (LINE or Telegram), when serialized, then the payload contains `media_type=sticker`, `mime_type` (image/webp or image/png), and `local_path` to the rasterized image — no metadata-based shortcut fields are populated.
- [ ] Given an animation/GIF, when serialized, then the payload contains `media_type=animation` and `local_path` to the first frame extracted as a static image.
- [ ] Edge case: Given an unsupported attachment type (e.g. video > 10s, document, contact card), when received, then the Gateway logs a structured warning and forwards a text placeholder to the Brain without raising an error.

---

### Task: Telegram inline media handler

**Requirement:** The Telegram module must download and forward photo, voice, audio, sticker, and animation update types into the unified Attachment pipeline.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given a Telegram update containing a photo, when received, then the highest-resolution variant is downloaded to `omni-workspace`, an entry is written to `file_workspace_log`, and a `StandardMessage` with `media_type=image` is dispatched.
- [ ] Given a Telegram voice update (`voice` field, OGG/Opus), when received, then the audio file is downloaded as-is without transcoding, logged, and dispatched with `media_type=voice` and correct `duration_ms` from the update payload.
- [ ] Given a Telegram sticker update, when received, then the sticker's static representation (webp or first frame for animated stickers) is downloaded and dispatched with `media_type=sticker`.
- [ ] Given a Telegram animation (GIF) update, when received, then the first frame is extracted as a still image and dispatched with `media_type=animation`.
- [ ] Edge case: Given a Telegram media file that exceeds the 10MB soft limit established in Phase 4B, when received, then the Gateway responds to the user with a polite size-limit message and does not enqueue the file.

---

### Task: LINE inline media handler

**Requirement:** The LINE module must download and forward image, audio, and sticker message types into the unified Attachment pipeline, with stickers routed through vision (no metadata lookup).

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given a LINE image message, when received, then the image is fetched via the LINE content endpoint, written to `omni-workspace`, logged, and dispatched as `media_type=image`.
- [ ] Given a LINE audio message, when received, then the audio file (m4a) is downloaded and dispatched as `media_type=voice` with `duration_ms` populated from the LINE event.
- [ ] Given a LINE sticker message, when received, then the sticker's static PNG (constructed from `packageId` + `stickerId` via LINE's CDN URL pattern) is downloaded and dispatched as `media_type=sticker` — no `packageId`/`stickerId` metadata is included as a shortcut, the Brain treats it identically to a Telegram sticker.
- [ ] Edge case: Given a LINE sticker whose CDN URL returns 404 (deprecated pack), when received, then the Gateway falls back to a text placeholder `[sticker: unavailable]` and logs the failure.

---

### Task: ModelRouter modality dimension

**Requirement:** The Brain's ModelRouter must treat modality as a routing dimension equal in priority to complexity. Voice and image inputs must force selection of a Gemini model, bypassing the Claude/local fallback chain.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given an incoming message with any non-text attachment, when the ModelRouter selects a model, then the result is always a Gemini variant (Flash or Pro) regardless of complexity assessment outcome.
- [ ] Given a text-only message, when the ModelRouter selects a model, then the existing Phase 4A routing logic (complexity + fallback chain) applies unchanged.
- [ ] Given a multimodal message and a Gemini API failure (timeout, auth error, quota exhausted), when the router has exhausted Gemini retries, then the router does NOT downgrade to Claude or local — it raises a `MultimodalUnavailable` exception that the Messenger layer catches and converts to the honest fallback reply.
- [ ] Edge case: Given a message with both text and an attachment, when routed, then the modality flag wins — Gemini is selected even if the text alone would have been routed to local.

---

### Task: Two-stage vision flow

**Requirement:** Image inputs must first attempt OCR (Phase 4B fast path) and only escalate to Gemini multimodal vision when OCR yields insufficient information for the user's intent.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given an image message, when received by the Brain, then Stage 1 OCR runs first using the Phase 4B FileAnalyzer pipeline and produces a text extraction result.
- [ ] Given OCR returns substantive text and the user's accompanying message can be answered from that text alone, when Flash performs complexity assessment, then Stage 2 vision is NOT invoked and only the OCR text is included in the LLM context.
- [ ] Given OCR returns empty/whitespace-only output, OR Flash's complexity assessment determines the image content (not just text) is needed to answer, when escalation triggers, then Stage 2 sends the original image to Gemini multimodal and uses that response.
- [ ] Edge case: Given Stage 1 OCR fails entirely (corrupt image, unreadable format), when escalation logic runs, then Stage 2 is invoked directly without waiting for OCR retries.
- [ ] Edge case: Given Stage 2 is invoked and Gemini returns a vision response, when the response is logged, then both Stage 1 and Stage 2 outcomes are recorded for later cost/quality analysis.

---

### Task: Voice transcript persistence

**Requirement:** Voice messages must be transcribed via Gemini native audio and the transcript stored in PostgreSQL; the original audio file follows the standard 120-hour cleanup.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given a voice message, when processed by the Brain, then Gemini receives the audio natively (no STT preprocessing) and returns both a transcript and the conversational response.
- [ ] Given a successful transcription, when persisted, then a row is written to a new `voice_transcripts` table containing user UUID, source platform, original message ID, transcript text, audio file path, duration_ms, and created_at timestamp.
- [ ] Given the standard 120-hour `omni-workspace` cleanup runs, when it processes voice files, then the audio file is deleted but the corresponding `voice_transcripts` row is preserved indefinitely.
- [ ] Edge case: Given Gemini returns a transcript that is empty or only contains non-speech markers, when persisted, then the row is still created with a marker value (e.g. `[no_speech_detected]`) so Phase 5 preference learning sees the gap rather than silently missing the message.

---

### Task: Sticker-via-vision unified path

**Requirement:** All stickers (Telegram and LINE) must be interpreted by sending the sticker image to Gemini multimodal — no platform-specific metadata lookup paths.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given a sticker message from either platform, when processed by the Brain, then the sticker image is sent to Gemini with a prompt that elicits a semantic description (mood, object, intent).
- [ ] Given Gemini returns a semantic description, when used in conversation context, then the description (not the raw image reference) is what subsequent LLM turns see — e.g. `[sticker: a cat raising its paw, conveying greeting/hello]`.
- [ ] Given the same sticker is sent multiple times by the same user, when processed, then a per-sticker-asset cache (keyed by SHA-256 content hash — same hash format used by the content-hash placeholder system) MAY be used to skip re-sending to Gemini; if implemented, the cache TTL is at least 30 days. The `media_cache` table design is specified in Task: Content-hash placeholder for attachment messages.
- [ ] Edge case: Given a sticker that Gemini fails to interpret, when fallback runs, then the placeholder `[sticker]` is used and the conversation continues without a hard failure.

---

### Task: Content-hash placeholder for attachment messages

**Requirement:** When a user sends an attachment (image, voice, sticker, animation, or other media), the Brain must store a content-addressed hash placeholder in the conversation record rather than a null `content` field. This placeholder is both a human-readable reference in conversation history and the foundation for a future `media_cache` lookup table that enables sticker re-use and cross-session media recall.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given a message with an attachment and no text, when saved to the conversation, then the user message `content` is `[media_type:HASH]` (e.g. `[sticker:a3b4c5d6e7f8]`) rather than `null`.
- [ ] Given a message with both text and an attachment (captioned media), when saved, then the user message `content` is `"{text} [{media_type}:{hash}]"`.
- [ ] Given the same sticker file is received from two different users, when placeholders are generated, then both produce the same HASH — confirming the hash is content-addressed (SHA-256 of file bytes), not identity-addressed (file_id).
- [ ] Given the attachment file is unreadable at placeholder-generation time (e.g. already cleaned up), when the fallback runs, then the hash is derived from `file_id` and the placeholder is still generated without raising an error.
- [ ] Given the placeholder appears in conversation history, when Cindy processes a follow-up text message, then the LLM context includes the placeholder string so Cindy can reference what media was previously seen without re-reading the file.
- [ ] Given a future `media_cache` table is introduced with schema `(hash TEXT PK, media_type TEXT, description TEXT, sticker_id TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ)`, when populated from the placeholder index, then the same hash key used in conversation history resolves directly to the cached Gemini description — enabling both deduplication and outbound sticker re-use.

**Implementation notes:**
- Hash is SHA-256 of first 512 KB of file bytes, hex-truncated to 12 characters.
- `_media_placeholder()` helper lives in `brain/main.py` and is called before LangGraph invocation.
- The `media_cache` table is **not** created in Phase 4D — it is deferred to Phase 5 or Phase 5.5 when sticker sending and preference learning are addressed.

---

### Task: Honest fallback on multimodal failure

**Requirement:** When Gemini multimodal is unreachable for any reason, Cindy must reply with an honest, non-evasive message asking the user to provide text instead — no silent dropping, no fabricated responses.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given a Gemini multimodal call fails (after exhausting OAuth refresh + API key fallback from Phase 4C), when the Messenger layer responds, then the user receives a message in Cindy's voice such as "我這邊看不到/聽不到,可以打字告訴我嗎?" (or context-appropriate equivalent in the user's language).
- [ ] Given the fallback message is sent, when logged, then a structured event is recorded with the failure cause (auth, quota, network, content_filter) for ops monitoring.
- [ ] Given multiple consecutive failures within a short window (e.g. 5 in 10 minutes), when detected, then a HomeLab admin alert is sent via the Telegram admin channel.
- [ ] Edge case: Given the failure was a content filter rejection (Gemini refused the input), when responding, then the fallback message does NOT reveal "your image was filtered" — it uses the same generic "looks like I can't see this clearly" phrasing to avoid signalling filter behavior.

---

### Task: Proactive multimodal acknowledgment

**Requirement:** When Cindy receives any non-text input (image, voice, sticker, animation), she must send a fixed-template acknowledgment message before initiating the full multimodal processing pipeline. The acknowledgment is a courtesy signal of social presence, not a technical confirmation.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given any multimodal message is received, when the Messenger layer processes it, then a fixed-template acknowledgment is dispatched to the user within 2 seconds, before Gemini multimodal processing begins.
- [ ] Given the acknowledgment templates exist, when reviewed, then there are at least 3 modality-specific templates: one for image/sticker/animation ("嗯,收到了,正在看..."), one for voice ("嗯,收到了,正在聽..."), and one generic fallback for unknown media types.
- [ ] Given the acknowledgment is sent, when the full multimodal response is later delivered, then the two messages appear as separate messages to the user (not edited into one).
- [ ] Given an acknowledgment is dispatched, when logged, then the original_message_id and acknowledgment_message_id are linked for traceability.
- [ ] Edge case: Given Gemini multimodal subsequently fails and the honest fallback message is sent, when delivered, then the user sees: ack message → fallback message ("可以打字告訴我嗎?") — never the ack alone with no follow-up.
- [ ] Edge case: Given the user sends multiple multimodal messages in rapid succession (e.g. 5 photos within 3 seconds), when processed, then ack messages are NOT sent for each — a single ack covers the batch (debounced, e.g. 5-second window).

---

### Task: GIF first-frame prompt hint

**Requirement:** When the Brain sends a GIF first frame to Gemini multimodal, the prompt template must explicitly inform Gemini that the image is a single frame extracted from an animation, so that interpretation is appropriately hedged.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given a `media_type=animation` attachment reaches the Brain, when the Gemini prompt is constructed, then it includes the hint "這是 GIF 首幀,全貌可能不同" (or context-appropriate language equivalent) directly adjacent to the image content block.
- [ ] Given a non-animation image is sent, when the Gemini prompt is constructed, then this hint is NOT included (only animations get the hint).
- [ ] Edge case: Given a sticker that is technically animated (Telegram animated sticker, first frame extracted), when sent to Gemini, then the same first-frame hint is included since the same caveat applies.

---

## Non-Functional Requirements

### Task: Latency budget for multimodal pipeline

**Requirement:** Multimodal message handling end-to-end should not exceed reasonable interactive latency.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given a typical voice message under 30 seconds, when processed end-to-end (download → Gemini → reply), then the user receives a response within 15 seconds under normal network conditions.
- [ ] Given an image message, when processed via Stage 1 OCR fast path only, then total response time is within 8 seconds.
- [ ] Given an image message that escalates to Stage 2 vision, then total response time is within 20 seconds.
- [ ] Edge case: Given any single stage exceeds its individual budget by 2x, when detected, then a structured warning is logged for tuning analysis (no user-facing change).

---

### Task: Storage hygiene

**Requirement:** Media files must follow the same lifecycle discipline as Phase 4B file uploads.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given any inline media is downloaded, when written, then it lands under `/workspace` with a path that includes the source platform and a UUID-based filename to avoid collisions.
- [ ] Given the existing 120hr cleanup task runs, when it scans `file_workspace_log`, then voice/image/sticker/animation files are eligible for deletion under the same last-access rule.
- [ ] Given a `voice_transcripts` row exists for a deleted audio file, when the file is removed, then the row remains and its `audio_path` column is updated to NULL or a tombstone marker.

---

## Integration Requirements

### Task: Gemini OAuth client multimodal calls

**Requirement:** The Phase 4C OAuthGeminiClient must be exercised for all multimodal calls, including the SELECT FOR UPDATE refresh path and API key fallback on OAuthRefreshError.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given a multimodal call with a valid OAuth token, when made, then it succeeds without falling back to API key.
- [ ] Given a multimodal call where the OAuth token is expired, when made, then DB-backed refresh runs (with SELECT FOR UPDATE), the call retries with the new token, and succeeds.
- [ ] Given OAuthRefreshError is raised, when caught, then the call falls back to API key and a warning is logged.
- [ ] Edge case: Given both OAuth and API key fail, when the failure propagates, then the honest fallback path (above) is invoked.

---

### Task: Schema migration for voice_transcripts

**Requirement:** A new PostgreSQL table `voice_transcripts` must be introduced with appropriate indexes and foreign keys.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given the migration is applied, when `\d voice_transcripts` is run, then the table exists with columns: id (UUID PK), user_uuid (FK to users), source_platform, source_message_id, transcript TEXT, audio_path TEXT NULL, duration_ms INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ.
- [ ] Given the migration is applied, when indexes are inspected, then there is an index on (user_uuid, created_at DESC) for Phase 5 preference learning queries.
- [ ] Given the migration is rolled back, when re-applied, then the operation is idempotent.

---

## Testing Notes

> Environment-specific instructions for Antigravity QA before verifying acceptance criteria.

- ⚙️ Use `podman compose` (not `docker compose`) for all container orchestration commands.
- ⚙️ Tests must run inside the HomeLab network so Caddy HTTPS gateway and webhook delivery from Telegram/LINE work end-to-end.
- ⚙️ The `omlx` local LLM service should be skipped in CI by setting `OMNI_ENV=test`; Gemini multimodal tests require a valid OAuth token in the `oauth_tokens` table OR a `GEMINI_API_KEY` env var as fallback.
- ⚙️ For LINE testing, use the official LINE Bot SDK simulator or a dedicated test channel — not a production family channel.
- ⚙️ For Telegram testing, use a dedicated test bot token; the admin/HomeLab channel must NOT receive test traffic.
- ⚙️ Voice transcript tests require Gemini audio support to be live — confirm the API contract in the Anthropic-equivalent Gemini documentation hasn't changed before running.
- ⚙️ The `omni-workspace` shared volume must be writable by both Gateway and Brain containers; verify with a touch test before media tests.
- ⚙️ BlueBubbles container should remain running but tests must NOT exercise its multimodal paths — explicit text-only assertions are required to confirm the freeze holds.

---

## Open Questions

> All Phase 4D open questions have been resolved by Icekimo prior to handoff. Resolutions are folded into the relevant tasks above. This section is intentionally empty — Jules should not block on any of the items below.

**Resolved decisions (for traceability):**
- **Sticker per-asset cache**: NOT implemented in 4D. Each sticker re-calls Gemini. Cache may become a Phase 4D.1 optimization if cost becomes a concern.
- **GIF first-frame prompt hint**: Brain prompt template MUST include the hint "這是 GIF 首幀,全貌可能不同" (or English equivalent) when sending an animation first frame to Gemini. See additional task below.
- **Proactive multimodal ack**: Cindy MUST send a fixed-template acknowledgment before processing multimodal input. See additional task below. The choice is fixed-template (not LLM-generated) to keep 4D scoped to perception, leaving personality expression to Phase 5 / Phase 6.
